module.name: server
module.purpose: Owns server task, plan, and step orchestration, including model-step prompt construction from request context and completed tool-call output.

childModules.name: invocation
childModules.purpose: Owns server-side invocation lifecycle and runtime routing between step events and host executor events.

childModules.name: model
childModules.purpose: Owns the server model backend abstraction. backend.rs defines the public ModelBackend trait, the crate-internal ModelBackendImpl trait, and a blanket impl that delegates request directly to ModelBackendImpl::request; backend_deepseek.rs implements DeepseekBackend as a config-driven streaming Deepseek chat-completions backend over reqwest with SSE parsing and a user-role single-message payload that preserves request.prompt as content; error.rs defines ModelBackendError with From conversions for io, serde_json, and reqwest errors plus a Display impl. mod.rs is namespace and re-export wiring only.

childModules.name: plan
childModules.purpose: Owns server-side plan creation and step orchestration, including model-input JSON built from plan background and completed call output before the model step starts.

childModules.name: prompt
childModules.purpose: Defines prompt rendering plus InitialPrompt and AnalysisPrompt construction from the request, background, call output, and serialized session tool previews.

childModules.name: relay
childModules.purpose: Owns server-side model relay lifecycle, streaming model responses into step updates and completion events.

childModules.name: session
childModules.purpose: Owns authenticated client and host session workers, hierarchical event routing, executor tool registration, task creation, and disconnect cleanup.

childModules.name: step
childModules.purpose: Owns server-side step dispatch and model request construction, including strict Analysis input JSON extraction for background and call output.

childModules.name: task
childModules.purpose: Owns task lifecycle, shared access to plans, steps, invocations, and relays, plus runtime dispatch across hierarchical protocol events.
